spec_match.rationale: Great conceptual formulas, but the active style block contains broken background color calc rules that cause visual rendering to fail.

interactive.rationale: Excellent interactive controls that dynamically rebuild the chart elements and compute and display mathematical values.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Accurately illustrates the performance and maintenance benefits of moving dynamic sizing math from JS to CSS.

edge_cases.rationale: Includes custom support validation and fallback to flat layout styles if the tree-counting APIs are missing.

references.rationale: References the correct ChromeStatus feature link and local sibling-index index page.

design_system.rationale: Utilizes standard grids, paper cards, and flex alignments according to guidelines.

openQuestions.title: Fix syntax-broken CSS background formulas in charts
openQuestions.detail: Lines 36 and 51 in css-only-charts/index.html contain corrupted CSS background formulas with mismatched parentheses and raw syntax errors. Fix them to correctly use HSL calc notation: `background: hsl(calc((var(--i) - 1) * 360deg / var(--n)) 70% 45%);` and `background: hsl(calc(210 - (var(--i) - 1) * (180 / var(--n))) 60% 40%);`.

summary: An excellent conceptual and highly interactive CSS-only data viz demo that lets users dynamically append elements to see the bars and polar charts adjust. However, severe syntax errors in the active CSS background colors ruin the visual output.
